commit_scope: Move the Bali living compound public entry path into a typed web registry so home and /come-in render the route from one source of truth instead of fixed page strings.

commands: cd web && npm test -- tests/bali-land-path-static.test.ts tests/homepage-presence-lineage.test.ts tests/aligned-communities-static.test.ts (first pass found missing fresh-worktree dependency: vitest)
cd web && npm test -- tests/bali-land-path-static.test.ts tests/homepage-presence-lineage.test.ts tests/aligned-communities-static.test.ts
for f in web/messages/*.json; do jq empty "$f" || exit 1; done
Playwright rendered local / and /come-in and found the registry-fed Bali land path copy
local_validation.summary: The Bali living compound entry now lives in web/lib/entry-paths.ts with kind, audiences, excluded kinds, surfaces, href, tone, and surface-specific copy. Home and /come-in render that entry through getEntryPathForSurface. The static guard now validates the registry, the page imports, locale keys, route resolvability, and excluded hospital/care-institution kinds instead of depending on hardcoded page href strings.

ci_validation.details: Pending push, PR checks, and merge verification.

deploy_validation.details: Public web verification will be performed after merge and deploy.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: A future entry path can be moved by changing the registry instead of editing each public page. The Bali land path remains visible from home and /come-in while keeping Bumi Sehat out of land-design and community-living slots.
test_flows: registry contains bali-living-compound as land-design-draft for home-ground, come-in-explore, and come-in-doors
home and /come-in source render through getEntryPathForSurface instead of hardcoded href strings
local browser rendering sees Brahmavihara Living Compound, Walk through /silence/built, Meet the land draft, and the localized lower /silence/built door

phase_gate.reason: Local proof is complete; commit, push, PR, checks, merge, and public verification remain pending.
